title: HTML Link Extractor
shortDescription: Extract href values, URLs, anchor text and rel data from raw HTML in your browser.
metaTitle: HTML Link Extractor | Extract href and URLs from HTML
metaDescription: Extract href values, URLs and anchor text from HTML online. Useful for href extractor, extract links from HTML and extract URLs from HTML file searches.
intro: Use this HTML link extractor when you need to pull href values, anchor text and rel attributes out of raw markup. It fits search intent around href extractor, extract links from HTML file and extract URLs from HTML without opening a crawler or parser locally.
overview: Paste HTML and get a structured list of links with href, visible text, rel values and target attributes. The tool is useful for SEO audits, internal-link reviews, migrations, QA checks and quick inspection of copied source code from templates, CMS output or live pages.
howItWorks: Paste the HTML snippet, full source or template fragment you want to inspect.
The extractor scans the markup for anchor tags and collects href, text, rel and target values.
Review the output list and copy the extracted link inventory for audits, QA or documentation.
useCases: Extract href values from raw HTML quickly.
Review anchor text for SEO and content audits.
Check rel attributes such as nofollow, sponsored and ugc.
Pull links out of template code before a migration.
Turn copied markup into a clean link inventory.

examples.title: Href extractor for a single link
examples.output: href: https://example.com | text: Visit site | rel: nofollow
examples.description: Helpful when you want a fast href extractor for one or many links.

examples.title: Extract links from copied source
examples.description: Useful for extracting internal links from copied page source or template HTML.

faq.question: Can this tool extract URLs from HTML files and snippets?
faq.answer: Yes. Paste the HTML source and the tool will list the URLs, anchor text and related attributes it finds.

faq.question: Is this the same as an href extractor?
faq.answer: Yes. It performs the same job and also shows extra link context such as anchor text, rel and target values.

faq.question: Can I use it for internal-link audits?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful for quick internal-link reviews, migration QA and copied-markup audits where you need a clean list of links fast.
